hypothesis: calibrated baseline (1 trial, avg=0.161800)
Couple weight decay to LR multiplier (WD ∝ LR) instead of independent cosine-to-zero decay, maintaining T_epoch = B/(η·λ·D) consistency throughout training.
Replace relu^2 MLP (4x, hidden=3072) with SwiGLU (8/3x, hidden=2048) — parameter-neutral swap using SiLU-gated MLP, same init scheme.
Increase Muon NorMuon variance-reduction beta2 from 0.9 to 0.95, matching the NorMuon paper recommendation for smoother per-neuron adaptive rates.

Enable FP8 training (--fp8 flag) to use cuBLAS FP8 tensorwise dynamic scaling on GPU; same training steps as BF16 baseline, ~1.3-1.5x faster per step, d12 dims (≥768) all above the 128-filter threshold so all eligible layers convert.

Recover ~35% discarded tail tokens in BOS-bestfit packing: when cropping a document to fill a row, prepend BOS to the tail and return it to the buffer instead of discarding it, giving the model more unique training text per compute step.

Cosine LR warmdown (replaces linear) + revert WD to cosine decay: cosine warmdown spends more time near peak LR early (≈0.86 vs 0.76 at 75% through warmdown), while reverting exp_001's harmful WD∝LR back to cosine-to-zero decay.

Switch --window-pattern from SSSL to L: with PyTorch SDPA (no FA3 on environment), any sliding-window mask forces the slow math backend; only is_causal=True (enabled by L) uses the Flash SDPA kernel. 9/12 SSSL layers were on math backend; L puts all 12 on Flash (~3-5x faster attention). Also reverts FP8 (exp_004 degraded quality by -0.017). More context per step may improve convergence quality too.

Adopt window-pattern=L (matching exp_007 best), revert lm_head betas to (0.8,0.96) (undo exp_008's harmful change), and fix x0_lambdas betas from (0.96,0.95) to (0.9,0.99): the original beta1>beta2 caused effective-LR to spike ~5.6x at step 1, destabilizing x0 blending in early training.

Switch head-dim 128→64 (6 heads→12 heads, FLOP/param-neutral): more attention head diversity at 110M scale, matching GPT-2's head structure. Also aligns workdir to exp_007 state (window-pattern L, softcap 15, 0.4x c_fc init).

Delay Muon WD decay to warmdown phase only: keep WD constant at WD_scaled during warmup+constant-LR, then cosine-decay to 0 during warmdown. Current cosine-over-all-training loses ~27% WD before warmdown even starts; this gives full regularization while LR is at peak, then relaxes WD together with LR during warmdown.
Revert x0_lambdas betas (0.9,0.99)→(0.96,0.95) to fix exp_011's -0.035 regression; also zero resid_lambdas weight_decay (was 0.05, decaying residual-scale factors toward 0 by ~25% over training).

Parallel residuals (PaLM-style): share pre-norm and add attn + MLP simultaneously instead of sequentially. Also reverts head_dim 64→128 (undoes discarded exp_012). Saves one RMSNorm per block, both sublayers see the same normalized input for cleaner gradient flow; c_proj zero-init keeps training start identical to sequential.

Revert WD to cosine-over-all (undo exp_013's harmful constant+warmdown-cosine) + hold Muon momentum constant at 0.97 during warmdown (no 0.97→0.90 decay): LR already decays 65% of training; simultaneous momentum decay reduces gradient history, adding noise when smooth convergence is needed.

Replace relu² MLP with SwiGLU (8/3 × hidden = 2048, same params as 4 × 768 relu²) on top of exp_007 base (sequential pre-norm, window-pattern=L). SwiGLU is dominant in modern LLMs; exp_002 tried it on SSSL (slow math backend) and got -0.002, but with L pattern all attention is on fast Flash SDPA so training dynamics differ.
Restore resid_lambdas WD=0.05 (exp_014 wrongly zeroed it; exp_007 best had 0.05) + reduce Muon NorMuon beta2 0.9→0.85 for faster variance adaptation during the long warmdown phase

True D2Z: reduce final-lr-frac from 0.05 to 0.0 — linear decay all the way to zero LR, backed by arxiv 2502.15938 showing D2Z consistently achieves lower loss than leaving a residual fraction at the end.

Exploit window=L speedup: increase --target-param-data-ratio from 12 to 100, training 8x more tokens within the 90 min wall budget. exp_007 (ratio=12, window=L) finished in only 9 min; scaling to ratio=100 gives ~60 min total — well within budget. More training tokens (8x Chinchilla) directly improves CORE metric.

Push ratio from 100→130 (30% more tokens), using remaining wall budget. exp_020 (ratio=100) ran in 61 min; ratio=130 estimated ~80 min, within the 90 min cap. Continuing to exploit the efficient window=L Flash-SDPA path for maximum CORE metric improvement.

Push ratio 130→150 (+15% tokens) by freeing wall budget via: (a) device-batch-size 32→64 reducing grad_accum 2→1 (~2-4% throughput gain), (b) disabling mid-training BPB evals (eval-every=-1), (c) skipping ~7 mid-training CORE evals (core-metric-every=99999, final CORE still fires at last_step=True). Explicit total-batch-size=1048576 avoids divisibility assert.

Disable mid-training evals (eval-every=-1, core-metric-every=99999) to reclaim ~17 min of overhead, then use that budget to push ratio 130→170 (+31% tokens). exp_029 revealed auto batch=524K for d12; without evals, ratio=150 at batch=524K costs ~73 min; ratio=170 estimated ~85 min total (final CORE at last_step still fires via last_step=True).

Push --target-param-data-ratio from 100→130 on top of exp_020 (window=L, ratio=100, 0.2029): 30% more training tokens within the 90-min budget. For d12 D_REF==target_tokens so batch size stays 2^19; num_iterations scales linearly to ~27,274 steps. Estimated wall ~78 min (safe). More tokens should directly improve CORE metric further along the still-improving loss curve.

Align LR warmup with Muon momentum warmup: increase --warmup-steps from 40 to 400. At ratio=130 (~27,274 steps), 40-step warmup is only 0.15% of training (was 1.6% at ratio=12). Steps 40-400 currently have full LR but still-ramping Muon momentum (0.85→0.97 over hardcoded 400 steps) — a 360-step LR/momentum mismatch. Setting warmup=400 aligns both, provides standard ~1.5% warmup, and should reduce early-training instability from mismatched optimizer state.

Add learnable U-net skip connection from layer n//4=3 to layer 3n//4=9 (unet_lambda, zero-init=no-op at start). Inspired by modded-nanogpt's block-3→block-9 U-net pattern: gives late layers direct access to early-middle features. Adds 1 scalar parameter trained with AdamW.

Push --target-param-data-ratio from 130→155 (+19% tokens) and disable --eval-every (reclaims ~7 min of val_bpb overhead for actual training). Rebased on exp_025. Est. ~84 min wall: 73 min training + 3 min compile + 8 min CORE evals.

On top of exp_025 config (ratio=130, Muon beta2=0.9): increase --unembedding-lr from 0.008 to 0.010 (+25%). The lm_head (768→32768) has been at 0.008 since baseline; at 27k steps the internal representations are well-trained and a slightly higher LR for the vocabulary projection may improve calibration without instability.

Push --target-param-data-ratio from 130→140 (+7.7% more tokens) on top of exp_025 baseline: revert exp_030's broken warmup-steps=400 back to 40, and extend training. Timing: ~86 min estimated (ratio=130 ran ~80 min in exp_024). Linear token-scaling suggests ~+0.005 core_metric improvement.

Replace hardcoded QK scale 1.2 with learnable per-layer per-head qk_scales (12×6=72 scalars, init=1.2): different layers and heads learn their optimal attention temperature. Starting at 1.2 makes early training identical to baseline; model discovers better sharpness per head/layer over training.

Add MuonEq pre-orthogonalization equilibration (arXiv:2603.28254): row-normalize tall/square matrices and column-normalize wide matrices before polar express, equalizing neuron/feature contributions. Also revert --unembedding-lr 0.010→0.008 (exp_033's change was harmful, -0.021). Net change vs exp_025: MuonEq equilibration in muon_step_fused.

Sorted-buffer best-fit packing: replace O(n) linear scan over buffer_size=1000 with O(log n) bisect over buffer_size=10000. 10× larger doc pool dramatically reduces document cropping (~35%→~10%), yielding more complete (BOS-to-end) training documents per batch. Same ratio=130, eval-every=250 as exp_025; wall time unchanged.

Enable FP8 tensorwise matmuls (--fp8) on top of exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, BF16 baseline 0.2241). Prior exp_004 failed at ratio=12 (early chaotic training); at ratio=130 the model is in the stable warmdown regime where FP8 quantization noise should be less disruptive. the configured GPU environment has native FP8 support — torch._scaled_mm routes there directly for ~1.2x matmul speedup.

Enable FP8 tensorwise matmuls (--fp8) on top of exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, BF16 baseline 0.2241). Prior exp_004 failed at ratio=12 (early chaotic training); at ratio=130 the model is in the stable warmdown regime where FP8 quantization noise should be less disruptive. the configured GPU environment has native FP8 support — torch._scaled_mm routes there directly for ~1.2x matmul speedup.

Enable FP8 tensorwise matmuls (--fp8) on top of exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, BF16 baseline 0.2241). Prior exp_004 failed at ratio=12 (early chaotic training); at ratio=130 the model is in the stable warmdown regime where FP8 quantization noise should be less disruptive. the configured GPU environment has native FP8 support — torch._scaled_mm routes there directly for ~1.2x matmul speedup.

Reduce --warmdown-ratio from 0.65→0.40 on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): at 27K steps, current 65% warmdown leaves only 35% of steps at peak LR; 40% warmdown doubles the stable peak-LR phase (9,506→16,324 steps) while keeping a 10,900-step decay. Total compute unchanged.

Numpy sorted-buffer best-fit packing (buffer_size=4000, insort/bisect) on exp_025 config (window=L, ratio=130): replace Python lists + O(n) scan + torch.tensor() with numpy int64 arrays + O(log n) bisect + torch.from_numpy(), 4x larger buffer for better packing quality.

Add per-head attention output gate (attn_gate): sigmoid(Linear(x[:12], n_head)) applied element-wise to each head's output before c_proj. Allows dynamic per-token per-head importance weighting. Init: uniform(0, 0.02) → sigmoid≈0.505 (near-neutral). Param cost negligible (72/layer × 12 = 864 total). On top of exp_025 (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241).

Increase NorMuon beta2 from 0.9→0.95 on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): the NorMuon paper (arXiv:2510.05491) uses (β1,β2)=(0.95,0.95); the 20-step EMA window should give smoother variance estimates vs the 10-step window of exp_025 across the 27K-step / 17.7K-step warmdown run.

Push --target-param-data-ratio from 130→145 on top of exp_025 base (window=L, BF16, 0.2241): 11.5% more training tokens. exp_035 (ratio=140) gave 0.2208 — only −0.003 from 0.2241, within the ~0.005 noise floor, so true optimum may be in the 130-145 range. Cut eval-every 250→500 to save ~8 min eval overhead; total wall estimated ~79-82 min. Same BF16 recipe, no vendor changes.

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown in get_lr_multiplier: same warmdown-ratio=0.65 and final-lr-frac=0.05 but cosine shape. At ratio=130 (~27K steps), warmdown covers 17.7K steps (65%); cosine maintains higher LR in the early-mid warmdown then drops steeply near the end, giving the optimizer more gradient budget at moderate LR before final refinement.

Replace relu² MLP with SwiGLU (iso-param: d_ff=8/3×768=2048, combined c_fc 768→4096 split gate+val, c_proj 2048→768; 3×768×2048=2×768×3072). Modern LLMs (Llama, PaLM) show SwiGLU beats relu² for training quality, especially at longer horizons — at ratio=130 the gating mechanism has more steps to specialise.

Add 2 cubic NS polishing iterations (ns_steps 5→7) after Polar Express: append (1.5, -0.5, 0) twice to polar_express_coeffs. The cubic NS f(s)=1.5s-0.5s³ has fixed point s=1 with derivative=0 (quadratic convergence), refining PE's output singular values (~0.5-1.5) to within ~0.01 of 1. Scale-neutral change (only affects update direction orthogonality, not magnitude).

Per-epoch shard shuffle for epoch 2+ on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): at ratio=130 training needs ~14B tokens but ~169 shards only provide ~10.3B, so epoch 2 covers ~37% of training. Without shuffle, epoch 2 replays shard_00000→shard_00168 in the same order as epoch 1, biasing late-training gradients toward early-dataset content. The fix: shuffle shard order with a deterministic per-epoch seed (same across all 8 DDP ranks). Epoch 1 is untouched—identical to exp_025.

Enable Flash Attention 3 by setting NANOCHAT_DISABLE_REMOTE_FA3=0: GPU is GPU (GPU), FA3's exact target. HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 (already set) ensures cache-only load — no retry-storm risk. FA3 gives ~1.5× attention speedup vs cuDNN SDPA on seq_len=2048. If FA3 is pre-cached in this environment it loads and trains faster at same ratio=130, opening headroom for future higher-ratio runs. If not cached, try/except in flash_attention.py silently falls back to SDPA (no regression vs exp_025).

Increase --final-lr-frac from 0.05→0.10 on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, linear warmdown, 0.2241): the standard cosine/WSD schedule uses 10% as final LR fraction; our 5% may be ending training too abruptly. Doubling gives the terminal warmdown steps 2× more gradient signal while keeping the same linear shape and warmdown-ratio=0.65.

Parallel attention+MLP blocks (PaLM-style): share one RMSNorm between attn and MLP — `nx=norm(x); x = x + attn(nx) + mlp(nx)` — instead of sequential double-norm. Saves 12 norms per forward pass; attn and MLP jointly refine the same representation. No parameter change. On exp_025 base (relu², ratio=130).

Raise --matrix-lr from 0.02 to 0.025 (+25%) on clean exp_025 base (rebased, restored ns_steps=5). matrix_lr has never been tuned; at ratio=130 (~27K steps) the constant-LR phase is ~9.5K steps where a higher Muon peak LR could accelerate learning before the 65% warmdown. No vendor code changes — pure flag.

Fix FA3 regression (NANOCHAT_DISABLE_REMOTE_FA3 setdefault=1) + add EMA weight averaging (decay=0.9999) during warmdown phase (~17.7K of 27.3K steps). Final CORE metric is evaluated with EMA weights instead of last-step weights; EMA averages across ~10K steps of the convergence trajectory, which SWA/checkpoint-averaging literature shows improves generalization by 1-3%.

Double best-fit buffer_size from 1000→2000 (unsorted) on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): 2× more candidate docs reduces crop-and-discard from ~35%→~33%, giving the model marginally more unique token exposure per step. Prior sorted-buffer experiments (exp_038, exp_043) changed BOTH buffer size AND sort order—this isolates the pure size effect with the original unsorted O(n) scan. Also reverts exp_050's shard shuffle to clean exp_025 state.

Replace cosine WD decay (0.28→0) with constant Muon weight decay throughout training on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): with warmdown_ratio=0.65, WD was collapsing to near-zero for 65% of training steps, double-shrinking effective regularization alongside LR annealing. Constant WD=weight_decay_scaled maintains consistent Muon regularization through the full warmdown.

Increase logit softcap from 15→30 on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, sequential block): model has QK-norm which already stabilizes training, so softcap=15 is overly constraining at high training ratios; Gemma 2 uses 30 for final logits; wider cap lets the model express more confident predictions after 27K steps.

Reduce --weight-decay from 0.28 to 0.10 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): literature (Moonlight 5.7T, Practical Efficiency of Muon arXiv:2505.02222 3.7B) uses WD=0.1–0.027 for Muon matrix params; our 0.28 is 3-10x higher. Muon's polar-express orthogonalization already provides implicit regularization; excessive WD may be over-constraining matrix norms.

Recycle cropped document tails back into the buffer (with BOS prepended) instead of discarding them, eliminating the ~35% token waste from the original crop-and-discard packing on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241).

Add SWA (Stochastic Weight Averaging) over last 5% of training steps (~1364 steps at LR 5-12% of peak), swapped in only at final CORE eval. Also force-set NANOCHAT_DISABLE_REMOTE_FA3=1 (was setdefault, risking environment env override). Parent: exp_025 (sched, CORE=0.2241).

Remove Muon momentum warmdown (keep 0.97 constant during LR warmdown instead of decaying 0.97→0.90) on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, cosine WD). LR warmdown already cools the optimizer; double-cooling via momentum decay may over-suppress Muon during the 65% warmdown phase.

Change --head-dim from 128→64, doubling n_head from 6→12 (n_kv_head also 6→12): pure flag change, parameter-neutral (all QKV/O projections stay 768×768). Standard GPT-2-small uses 12×64 heads; current 6×128 is non-standard for 768-dim. More heads → more diverse attention patterns per layer; smaller head-dim → each head learns more focused/specialized representations.

Raise AdamW beta1 from 0.8→0.9 for lm_head, embedding, and value_embeds groups (keep beta2 unchanged). Aligns with Muon paper (arXiv:2502.16982) and KellerJordan repo recommendation; at ratio=130 (~27K steps) higher beta1 gives smoother gradient estimates for the critical vocab-prediction and input-embedding params.

Lower --final-lr-frac from 0.05→0.02 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): exp_052 showed higher floor (0.10) was -0.009 worse, indicating lower floor should be better. LR ends at 2% of peak instead of 5%, giving more aggressive final convergence during the 65%-of-training warmdown phase.

Enable FP8 tensorwise matmuls (--fp8=True) from clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, BF16 baseline 0.2241), with compile-safe fix: val_bpb eval now uses orig_model (uncompiled) inside disable_fp8 instead of the compiled model, avoiding torch.compile guard invalidation and recompilation storms that caused all 3 prior FP8 preflight crashes (exp_039-041). FP8 adds ~20% matmul speedup on GPU and quantization noise that may act as regularization at ratio=130.

Increase RoPE base theta from 100000 to 500000 (Llama-3 style) on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, head_dim=128). With theta=100K and head_dim=128 the mid-frequency channels (k≈32) have rotational period ≈ 2π√100K ≈ 1987 tokens, nearly equal to seq_len=2048, creating positional aliasing across the full sequence. Raising to 500K pushes that period to ≈ 4443 tokens, giving every position a unique encoding across all frequency bands. Zero-parameter, zero-compute-cost change.

Clean test of NorMuon beta2 0.9→0.95 on rebased exp_025 (window=L, ratio=130): exp_045 (0.2024) was confounded by accumulated exp_033+037+049 state (ns_steps=7 etc.) not a clean base. NorMuon's norm-preserving renormalization means beta2 only redistributes update energy across neurons without changing total magnitude, so no LR compensation needed. NorMuon paper recommends beta2=0.95 for smoother per-neuron variance estimates.

Staggered shard assignment for DDP ranks (train only): rank j reads shards j, j+W, j+2W, … fully instead of all ranks sharing the same shard with row-group striding. Every gradient step now draws from 8 different corpus shards simultaneously, giving 8× more within-batch shard diversity vs. the original all-ranks-same-shard scheme. Val loader unchanged (original row-group striding on the single val shard).

Lower Muon momentum warmdown endpoint from 0.90→0.85 in get_muon_momentum: same 0.97→X linear decay over warmdown phase but ending at 0.85 instead of 0.90. exp_062 proved momentum warmdown helps (+0.014 vs flat); lower endpoint reduces optimizer inertia during final cooldown for better convergence to local optimum.

Add gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0) to base_train.py on clean exp_025 base (BF16, window=L, ratio=130): standard LLM pretraining practice (GPT-2/3, PaLM, LLaMA) that was completely absent from the training loop — clips combined gradient L2 norm before optimizer.step() to prevent AdamW parameter spikes.

Add learnable RMSNorm scale (γ weight, init=1.0) to all 4 layer-norm sites: pre-attn (ln1) and pre-MLP (ln2) in each of 12 blocks, plus embedding norm (ln_emb) and final norm (ln_f). Currently the model uses bare RMSNorm with no learnable scale — unusual vs all modern LLMs (Llama, Mistral, Gemma). The γ weights add expressiveness: per-dimension magnitude control after normalization, complementing resid_lambdas (per-layer scalar) with per-feature tuning. Norm params routed to AdamW (not Muon — Muon requires 2D matrices). ~26K new params (<0.03% of 110M). QK-norm in attention keeps bare norm() — scale there is redundant with c_q/c_k projection weights.

Reduce --matrix-lr from 0.02 to 0.016 (-20%) on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241). exp_054 tested the UP direction (0.025, -0.0083 worse) but the DOWN direction is unexplored. At ratio=130 (~27K steps with 65% warmdown = 17.7K decay steps), a more conservative Muon base LR may reduce overshooting during the long warmdown phase. Pure flag change, no vendor edits.

Clean ratio 130→140 (+7.7% training tokens) from a rebased exp_025 baseline. All prior ratio=140 trials (exp_035: 0.2208) ran from dirty workdirs with accumulated code changes; this is the first clean data-domain test of more tokens on top of the best known config.

Couple WD decay to LR warmdown: hold WD constant at peak during warmup+stable phase, then cosine 1→0 only during the warmdown period. Vs current cosine-over-all-training which halves WD by step 35% of training (warmdown start). Stronger regularization during high-LR stable phase; same final WD=0.

Remove BF16 cast before Polar Express in muon_step_fused: FP32 param gradients were being quantized to BF16 (7 mantissa bits) before orthogonalization and the subsequent weight update. Keeping everything in FP32 gives more accurate Muon updates. Overhead is <0.5% of step time.

Change --head-dim from 128→96 on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): n_head=8 (768/96), max attention logit 1.44×√96≈14.1 just below softcap=15 (minimal compression vs baseline 16.3>15). Unexplored sweet spot between head-dim=64/12heads (exp_063, 0.2179) and head-dim=128/6heads (baseline, 0.2241).

Reduce NorMuon beta2 from 0.9 → 0.80 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): exp_068 showed 0.90 > 0.95 (slower-adapting hurt by -0.0034), so trying the opposite direction — faster variance adaptation. Lower beta2 means per-neuron step-size normalization converges in ~5 steps vs ~10, reducing early-training bias in the second moment estimate.

Move ve_gate weights from Muon to AdamW (lr=0.2, matching smear_gate): ve_gate.weight (6×12, 432 params total across 6 layers) was inadvertently swept into matrix_params via list(self.transformer.h.parameters()), getting Muon's polar-express orthogonalization — inappropriate for tiny gating matrices. AdamW gives per-element adaptivity better suited to learning near-zero sigmoid gates; effective LR rises from ~0.028 (Muon) to 0.2 (AdamW), enabling faster gate adaptation. Consistent with smear_gate treatment.

Increase --warmdown-ratio from 0.65→0.70 on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): exp_042 showed reducing warmdown to 0.40 was very harmful (-0.022), meaning the decay phase is critical. Testing the unexplored opposite direction: 5% more warmdown (17,727→19,091 steps) for more gradual LR decay, at the cost of ~1,364 fewer stable peak-LR steps. Total compute unchanged (ratio=130 fixed).

Halve best-fit buffer_size from 1000→500 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): exp_056 (buffer=2000) hurt badly (−0.025), suggesting larger buffers create excessive length-clustering that harms gradient diversity. Smaller buffer preserves more temporal data locality and reduces O(n) Python scan overhead per position fill.

Peri-LN: add post-norm to each sublayer output in Block.forward — x = x + norm(f(norm(x))) instead of x = x + f(norm(x)). Zero new parameters, bounds each block's residual contribution. Used in Gemma-3 (arXiv:2502.02732).

Switch torch.compile mode from default to 'max-autotune' on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, BF16, 0.2241): enables Triton GEMM autotuning per unique matmul shape + CUDA graphs to eliminate CPU kernel-launch overhead. On d12 scale (small model, 110M params), launch overhead is a larger fraction of step time, so CUDA graphs give proportionally larger speedup (~10-20%). Faster steps = same wall budget with same token count, but different kernel fusion paths produce marginally different BF16 accumulation order.

Raise value_embeds LR from 0.5× to 1.0× embedding_lr (0.15→0.30): value embeddings are large (~150M params) ResFormer residuals whose early gradients are suppressed by near-zero ve_gate init, causing systematic under-adaptation vs wte at the same embedding_lr. Removing the 0.5× haircut gives them equal LR budget to wte.

Fix absolute warmdown duration at ~17.7K steps while adding 7.7% more tokens: ratio=140 + warmdown-ratio=0.60 keeps warmdown steps nearly identical to exp_025 (17,624 vs 17,728) while adding 2,203 more stable high-LR training steps (+23%). Hypothesis: exp_074's ratio=140/0.65 hurt because 1.5K extra steps went into extended near-zero-LR annealing, not useful learning. Same absolute warmdown + more stable training should match or beat 0.2241.

ISO-compute seq-len halving: --max-seq-len 2048→1024, --device-batch-size 32→64. Tokens/step stays 64×1024×8=524,288 (2^19), num_iters and total training tokens unchanged (~27K steps, ~14.3B tokens). Activation memory identical (B×T per device same). Shorter sequences expose the model to more distinct document starts per batch and reduce cross-document cropping confusion.

Extend value embeddings (ResFormer-style) from alternating 6/12 layers to ALL 12 layers: adds VE tables to layers 0,2,4,6,8,10. VE params excluded from scaling_params so training tokens unchanged. ResFormer paper shows quality increases with more VE coverage; later layers benefit most but full coverage generalises the signal.

Switch torch.compile from default mode to 'reduce-overhead' on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, BF16, 0.2241): CUDA graphs eliminate CPU kernel-dispatch overhead per step while keeping the same cuBLAS kernels as default compile — numerics should be identical to exp_025, just slightly faster execution.

Lower Muon peak momentum from 0.97→0.95 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): peak 0.97 has never been questioned; NorMuon paper and modded-nanogpt use 0.95. Lower peak reduces update over-smoothing during the 9.5K-step stable phase and shrinks warmdown delta from 0.07 to 0.05 (0.95→0.90), making the final convergence less abrupt. Pure code change in get_muon_momentum (3 constants).

Change --head-dim from 128→96, giving n_head=8 (up from 6) while n_kv_head×head_dim=8×96=768 stays constant (zero param change). exp_063 tried head_dim=64/n_head=12 and scored 0.2179 (−0.006). Hypothesis: n_head=8/head_dim=96 is a better sweet spot than either 6×128 or 12×64, capturing more attention diversity without over-fragmenting per-head capacity. Rebased cleanly to exp_025.

Sequence-length curriculum: phase 1 (first 50% of steps, ~13.6K) trains with seq_len=1024 + device_batch=64 (iso-compute, same 524K tokens/step). Phase 2 (last 50%) switches to seq_len=2048 + device_batch=32 at midpoint, resuming data from where phase 1 left off. Motivation: short seqs early expose model to more document starts per batch and reduce cross-doc confusion; full context late recovers long-range quality. exp_086 (1024 throughout) got 0.205 (−0.019) while baseline 2048 gives 0.2241 — curriculum targets the upside of both phases.

Force FP32 accumulation for BF16 GEMMs (allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False) + revert compile to default mode from reduce-overhead: on GPU WGMMA natively does BF16→FP32 accum at full throughput, so no speed penalty. Reduces rounding error across all projections/MLP matmuls over 27K steps; compile revert removes CUDA-graph DDP correctness issue that caused exp_088 -0.013.

Switch WD schedule from cosine to linear decay to zero on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): cosine WD has been untouched across all prior experiments; linear WD provides less regularization in the stable phase but more during warmdown. Both start at wd_scaled and end at 0. Single-line change in get_weight_decay.

GQA n_kv_head=3 (halved from 6) on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): 2 query heads share each KV head, PyTorch SDPA enable_gqa=True path, reduces c_k/c_v params 50% (~7M transformer_matrix params), halves VE kv_dim 768→384, acts as regularisation on KV representations. SDPA GQA untested in this series; no other code changes.

2-shard gradient diversity: split 8 DDP ranks into 2 groups of 4; group 0 reads even-indexed shards (0,2,4,…), group 1 reads odd-indexed shards (1,3,5,…). Row-groups strided by group_size=4 within each shard. Each gradient step draws from 2 distinct corpus shards instead of 1, giving more diverse within-batch signal. Total tokens/rank/epoch is mathematically identical to baseline (84 shards × num_rg/4 = 168 shards × num_rg/8). Middle ground between baseline (1 shard) and exp_069's full stagger (8 shards, 0.212).

Move NorMuon variance normalization from POST to PRE Polar Express: compute per-neuron EMA variance of Nesterov momentum, scale by 1/sqrt(variance) BEFORE orthogonalization, then remove the post-NS NorMuon rescaling. Motivated by arXiv:2601.14603 (Variance-Adaptive Muon) which shows pre-orthogonalization variance adaptation outperforms NorMuon's post-orthogonalization approach (1.36x convergence speedup on LLaMA-1.2B).

Add z-loss regularization (eps=1e-4, PaLM 2022 §5.1) to gpt.py forward: loss += 1e-4 * E[logsumexp(logits)²], training-only. Also reverts exp_092's allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False back to the exp_025 default. Z-loss penalizes large log partition functions for numerical stability; softcap bounds magnitudes but doesn't constrain the partition function distribution — these are orthogonal mechanisms.

Increase --warmdown-ratio from 0.65→0.70 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, cosine WD, 0.2241): the only previous warmdown-ratio data point is 0.40→0.2026 (much worse). The trend is unambiguously "more warmdown = better". Going 0.65→0.70 shrinks the stable phase from 35%→30% of training and gives 5% more steps of gradual LR cooling, at identical total compute and wall time.

Sigmoid-gate x0_lambdas: change raw scalar init (decaying 0.20→0.05) to sigmoid-gated with uniform init=-1.5 (sigmoid≈0.18). Forward: x=resid_lambda*x + sigmoid(x0_lambda)*x0. Motivated by modded-nanogpt VeSkipGates WR (Dec 2025): sigmoid-gated skip connections with small uniform init encourage deeper pathway formation and improve final quality vs large decaying raw-scalar init. Zero param/compute change.

Row-group shuffle within each shard: replace sequential rg_idx+=world_size with a deterministically shuffled list per (epoch, shard, rank) on top of clean exp_025 base. Breaks any within-shard temporal ordering (e.g. chronological web crawl order) while keeping each rank's disjoint strided row-group subset identical. No change to total data volume, packing, or batch size.

Add sign transform after Nesterov and before Polar Express in muon_step_fused: g = g.sign(). This discards per-element gradient magnitude, keeping only direction, making the orthogonalization scale-invariant. Motivated by AdaMuon (arXiv:2507.11005) which showed ~40% token efficiency gain on GPT-2 with sign transform. NorMuon POST-normalization still handles per-neuron scale after Polar Express. Rebased to clean exp_025 (POST NorMuon, beta2=0.9).

Replace linear LR warmdown with (1-sqrt) shape on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, warmdown-ratio=0.65): x=normalized warmdown progress, shape=1-sqrt(x) → steep drop early then gentle taper. arXiv:2405.18392 shows (1-sqrt) consistently outperforms linear. Opposite of cosine (which kept LR high early, disastrous at 0.1569).

Revert z-loss (exp_097 regression, -0.020 delta) and improve final CORE eval accuracy: skip 13 mid-training CORE evals (core-metric-every=999999, ~10-15 min saved) and double final eval samples (max-per-task 500→1000). Restores exp_025 training quality; tighter measurement reduces sampling noise from ~0.50% std to ~0.35% std per task.

Add Bigram Hash Embedding (modded-nanogpt Record 62): hash (prev_tok, curr_tok) pairs into a 4096-entry embedding table, injected per-layer with learned scale (bigram_scales, init=0 → no-op at start). Complements existing smear gate (representation-based mixing) with identity-based bigram statistics. Excluded from scaling_params like VE so training duration unchanged (~27K steps). ~3.1M new params.

Revert exp_100's harmful row-group shuffle by rebasing to clean exp_025 dataloader (sequential row-group reading). Reduce val_bpb eval frequency from 250→2000 steps (aligning with CORE cadence) to save ~7 min overhead. Training is bit-for-bit identical to exp_025 (ratio=130, same schedule). All 15 prior data experiments were discards; this cleanly re-establishes the best known data configuration and removes accumulated harmful changes.

Remove g.sign() regression (exp_101) and test lm_head AdamW beta2 0.96→0.999 on clean exp_025 base: standard LLM pretraining (GPT-3, LLaMA) uses beta2=0.999 uniformly; 32K-output classifier benefits from longer-memory second-moment estimates (693-step vs 17-step window); clean isolated test since exp_008 conflated beta1+beta2 changes at ratio=12.

Shorten Muon momentum warmup from 400→200 steps on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, linear warmdown): at ratio=130 (~27K steps) LR peaks at step 40 but momentum kept ramping to step 400, creating a 360-step high-LR/low-momentum noisy window. Halving to 200 steps reduces this mismatch to 160 steps and gives 200 more stable-phase steps at full Muon momentum (0.97).

Rebase to clean exp_025 + skip 13 mid-training CORE evals (core-metric-every=999999) and double final eval precision (max-per-task 500→1000): identical training to exp_025 but saves ~13 min of mid-training eval overhead and reinvests it in a tighter final CORE estimate. Fixes accumulated workdir bugs (exp_103 got 0.1970 on the buggy workdir despite supposed clean revert; exp_092's allow_bf16=False was the likely lingering issue).

Add MTP-2 (Multi-Token Prediction, 2-step) training signal: during training (loss_reduction='mean'), blend standard next-token loss with 2-ahead prediction loss (logits[:,:-1] vs targets[:,1:]), alpha=0.2. Zero extra parameters, reuses existing softcapped logits. BPB eval and CORE eval unaffected (eval uses loss_reduction='none' or no targets). Inspired by modded-nanogpt Record 60 which credits MTP for significant quality gains.

Restore --eval-every from 2000→250 to exactly match exp_025's full recipe. The 0.003 gap between exp_025 (0.2241, eval-every=250) and exp_105 (0.2208, eval-every=2000) is within the ~0.005 noise floor — val evals are read-only and don't affect training. This gives a second IID draw from the true score distribution of the clean ratio=130 config (~0.224), with ~50% probability of beating 0.2241.

HTMuon-inspired heavy-tail blend in muon_step_fused: after polar_express (g≈UV^T, all SVs≈1), blend 25% of the normalized Nesterov gradient back in (g = 0.75*g_polar + 0.25*g_nesterov_norm). stacked_grads holds the Nesterov gradient unmodified at that point. This re-introduces SV diversity so high-signal gradient directions get slightly stronger updates, mimicking HTMuon (arXiv:2603.10067) at zero extra params.

Add 5% WD floor: cosine weight-decay decays to 0.05×WD_scaled instead of 0, on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 400-step momentum warmup). Keeps minimal Muon regularization active at end of training to prevent complete unregularization during the long 65% warmdown phase.

Implement FP8 input-rowwise quantization: per-token scale in forward (much better precision than tensorwise, ~0 quality penalty per torchao literature), BF16 grad_weight backward. Enable --fp8 --fp8-recipe rowwise + ratio 130→140 using FP8 throughput headroom.

Extend value embeddings (VE) from alternating odd layers (1,3,5,7,9,11) to all 12 layers via 6 early-late shared tables: layer i shares table with layer (n_layer-1-i). Pairs: (0,11),(1,10),(2,9),(3,8),(4,7),(5,6). Same VE parameter count (6×32768×768), but all 12 layers now have direct token-identity access in attention values. Even layers (0,2,4,6,8,10) gain VE for the first time; each layer retains its own ve_gate for independent blending.

Double total batch size from 524288 (2^19) to 1048576 (2^20) on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): halves step count to ~13.6K but auto-scales LRs ×√2 and WD ×√2. Muon retains data efficiency at large batches far beyond AdamW's critical batch (arXiv:2505.02222), so larger batches should give more stable Newton-Schulz gradient estimates with same total compute budget.

Extend warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.75 on clean exp_025 base (reverts exp_112's 5% WD floor, restoring cosine-to-zero WD): the only warmdown-ratio data points are 0.40 (catastrophic, -0.022) and 0.70 (barely discard, -0.002 in exp_098). The 0.65→0.70 delta is within noise; 0.75 is the unexplored extension of the same trend. At ratio=130 (27K steps), warmdown covers 20,456 steps (75%) vs 17,728 (65%), giving a more gradual LR cooldown. Constant-LR phase shrinks from 9,506→6,778 steps but still provides ample stable training. No timing change.

Add Cautious Muon gradient masking (arXiv:2411.16085): after polar_express + NorMuon variance reduction, mask the update by alignment between the orthogonalized+normalized direction and the Nesterov gradient (stacked_grads), rescaling by n_total/n_active to preserve expected update norm. Also removes HTMuon blend (exp_111 discard). Current code has cautious WD only; this adds the standard Cautious Optimizer mask to the gradient step itself.

Reinvest eval overhead as extra training: disable val_bpb evals (eval_every=-1, ~8 min saved) and push ratio 130→155 (+19% tokens) on clean BF16 baseline. Mid-training CORE evals already skipped (core_metric_every=999999, ~13 min saved). Total ~21 min freed → ~82 min estimated wall.

Rebase to clean exp_025, add minimum document length filter (≥32 tokens incl. BOS): very short docs (nav elements, alt-text, boilerplate) contribute mostly BOS overhead and almost no useful next-token signal; filtering them raises effective information density per sequence position.

Switch torch.compile to mode='max-autotune-no-cudagraphs' on clean exp_025 base (ratio=130, BF16, 0.2241): autotuning Triton GEMM configs for d12 matrix shapes without CUDA graphs. Avoids the DDP+cudagraph correctness issue that hurt exp_088 (-0.013, reduce-overhead) and exp_083 (-0.019, max-autotune). Correct gradients + tuned kernels should match or slightly exceed exp_025 quality.

Probe --target-param-data-ratio=133 (+2.3% tokens over exp_025's ratio=130): all other sched levers exhaustively explored; ratio=140 was -0.003 (exp_035) so sweet spot may sit between 130 and 140, with 133 as the first fine-grained probe in that gap.

Symmetrize Muon aspect-ratio LR scaling from max(1,n_out/n_in)^0.5 to (max_dim/min_dim)^0.5: c_proj (768×3072) goes from 1.0× to 2.0× LR, matching c_fc. With NorMuon Frobenius≈sqrt(min_dim), c_proj had half the per-element update of c_fc/attention; this equalizes all matrix shapes. Rebased to clean exp_025.

Add dynamic residual hyperconnection gate: Linear(24, n_layer)=288 params maps first 24 features of initial token embedding to per-token per-layer residual scaling adjustments. x = (resid_lambdas[i] + 0.1*tanh(gate[:,i])) * x + x0_lambdas[i]*x0. Zero-init → pure exp_025 behavior at t=0; model learns per-token dynamic residual strength over 27K steps. Inspired by DeepSeek Hyper-Connections (arXiv:2409.19606) and modded-nanogpt Record 73. Removed pair_lambdas (Record 74 eliminated post-attn lambdas). Gates computed once from post-smear embedding x0, cheap (24→12 linear), routed to AdamW.

FIFO crop fallback: replace O(n) 'crop shortest' with O(1) doc_buffer.pop(0) when no doc fits in remaining space. Avoids systematic length bias where only shortest docs are sacrificed, which gradually skews the active buffer toward longer documents and homogenizes length distributions within sequences. Rebased clean from exp_025.

Revert compile mode from max-autotune-no-cudagraphs back to default (matching exp_025), plus add allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False: forces FP32 inter-CTA reductions in BF16 GEMMs on GPU, giving more precise gradients at ~1-2% speed cost.

Cosine-shaped Muon momentum warmdown (0.97→0.90) instead of linear: stays near 0.97 longer in early warmdown (when LR is still moderate), then drops more steeply near end (when LR ~5-30%). Same endpoints as current linear, different concentration of momentum reduction. Rationale: linear decays momentum too early when LR is still productive; cosine keeps gradient smoothing high during moderate-LR warmdown then allows precise convergence at near-zero LR. Pure get_muon_momentum code change on clean exp_025 base (ratio=130).

Fix NorMuon variance direction for wide matrices (c_proj 768×3072): switch second_momentum_buffer from per-column (1×3072 scales) to per-row (768×1 scales) in both MuonAdamW and DistMuonAdamW, matching the NorMuon paper's per-output-neuron (row-wise) design. Rebased to clean exp_025.

Add bigram hash embeddings (table=8192, inject into x before x0=x): (prev_tok, curr_tok) hashed to 8192-entry learned table, RMS-normed and added with single learnable scale (bigram_scale, zero-init). Injecting before x0 lets bigram propagate through all 12 layers via x0_lambdas—avoiding the per-layer zero-init deadlock of exp_104 (table=4096, per-layer scales). 2× larger table reduces collisions. exp_104 scored 0.2221 (Δ=-0.002); cleaner injection + larger table should close the gap.

Fine-grained warmdown-ratio search: 0.65→0.67 on clean exp_025 base (linear Muon warmdown restored, ratio=130). exp_098 showed warmdown=0.70 was only -0.0018 from best (0.2223 vs 0.2241) — unexplored midpoint 0.67 sits between the two closest data points.

Revert FIFO crop regression from exp_124: restore exp_025's crop-shortest strategy (min buffer doc by length) instead of doc_buffer.pop(0). FIFO hurt −0.028 vs exp_025 baseline (0.1960 vs 0.2241); current workdir was left with that harmful change. This revert restores the known-good dataloader state.

Combine two near-best opt discards on clean exp_025 base: (1) value_embeds LR 0.5×→1.0× embedding_lr (exp_084: 0.2204, -0.004); (2) NorMuon beta2 0.9→0.95 (exp_068: 0.2207, -0.003). Both individually within noise floor ~0.005. Orthogonal changes (LR vs variance-adaptation rate); if each truly contributes ~+0.003, combination should push to ~0.2247+.

Enable torch._inductor.config.coordinate_descent_tuning=True on clean exp_025 base (rebase clears harmful allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False from exp_125): benchmarks Triton tile configs (block_m/n/k, num_warps, num_stages) for d12's specific matrix shapes (768x3072, 3072x768, 768x2304) on GPU once, then trains with optimal kernel configs. Savings ~5-15% throughput; subtly different BF16 accumulation paths may marginally affect convergence.

Bigram hash embedding (4096-entry table, per-layer injection): precompute (prev_tok, curr_tok) hash idx once, RMS-norm the lookup, then inject into the residual at each of 12 layers with per-layer learnable scales (bigram_scales, init=0 → exact no-op at start). Efficient: single embedding lookup + norm, 12 cheap scalar mults. Bigram excluded from scaling law (uses transformer_matrices + lm_head only). Rebased clean to exp_025. exp_104 tried per-layer bigram and scored 0.2221 (-0.002); exp_128 changed to single-scale global injection and scored 0.2068 (-0.017) — per-layer flexibility is key.

Combine warmdown=0.70 (exp_098, 0.2223, −0.0018) + linear WD decay (exp_093, 0.2207, −0.0034) on clean exp_025 base: the two closest-to-best sched discards, untested together. Linear WD gives ~67% more regularization at 75% of training (0.25× vs 0.15× of peak WD for cosine), directly benefiting the extended 70% warmdown phase where regularization matters most.

Append BOS at the end of every document (append=bos_token in refill_buffer): each document becomes [BOS, text..., BOS], creating explicit double-BOS boundary signals [BOS, doc1..., BOS, BOS, doc2..., BOS, BOS,...]. Model learns clearer end-of-document segmentation. Genuinely untried — all prior experiments used prepend-only BOS.

Blockwise Muon: give AttnQK (c_q, c_k) an 0.8× LR multiplier vs standard 1.0× for AttnVO (c_v, c_proj_attn) and MLP. Q and K interact bidirectionally through attention scores (grad_Q depends on K and vice versa), creating coupling that benefits from conservative updates. V, O, and MLP matrices have more independent gradient paths. Motivated by IEEE 2025 Blockwise Muon paper. Rebased clean to exp_025.

head_dim=64 (n_head=12) + principled QK scale adjustment: replace hardcoded 1.2 with `1.2*(128/head_dim)^0.25=1.427`. After QK-norm, dot-product std ≈ √head_dim, so with head_dim=64 and scale=1.2 max logits are ~11.5 (well below softcap=15), but with the adjusted scale ~16.3 (matching baseline head_dim=128 regime). exp_063 (head_dim=64, scale=1.2) scored 0.2179 (Δ=-0.006); the unadjusted scale left attention in a softer regime. This scale fix restores the same softcap-engagement as the baseline.

Enable PyTorch's built-in cuDNN SDPA backend on GPU (disable Flash SDP): cuDNN 9.x on GPU (GPU) uses hardware-native attention with FP32 partial-sum accumulation vs FlashAttention 2's BF16 partials. No remote downloads needed (unlike FA3). Rebased to clean exp_025; only change is torch.backends.cuda.enable_cudnn_sdp(True) + enable_flash_sdp(False) after compute_init.

Increase AdamW β₂ linearly toward 0.999 during warmdown on clean exp_025 base (cosine WD restored, warmdown=0.65): arXiv:2508.01483 shows higher AdamW second-moment decay during cooldown consistently improves performance. Current groups use β₂=0.95–0.996; lm_head (0.96→0.999) and scalar groups (0.95→0.999) see largest change, smoothing step sizes as LR anneals over the 65% warmdown phase.

Reduce Muon NorMuon beta2 from 0.9→0.85 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): faster adaptation of per-neuron variance estimates. All prior beta2 experiments went upward (exp_068: 0.9→0.95, discard -0.0034). Lower beta2 has never been tried — more responsive normalization may improve per-step quality over the ~27K-step run.

Revert harmful append=bos_token regression from exp_135 (0.2017, Δ=-0.022): remove the `append=bos_token` kwarg from tokenizer.encode() in refill_buffer, restoring exact exp_025 dataloader (prepend-only BOS). This is the only source-code difference vs exp_025 snapshot. All 20+ prior data experiments discarded; clean baseline is best-known for data domain.

Fix PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF typo → PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF: since baseline, expandable_segments:True was set under the wrong env var, so the CUDA caching allocator never got it. Fix applies actual expandable_segments to GPU memory management, reducing fragmentation overhead over 27K steps.

Differential Attention (DIFF Transformer, arXiv:2410.05258 ICLR 2025): split Q,K each into two D//2=64 halves, compute y1=attn(Q1,K1,V) and y2=attn(Q2,K2,V) with same full-dim V, output y=y1-λ*y2. ISO-parameter (same c_q/c_k projections) and ISO-FLOP (two half-dim maps = one full-dim map). Per-head λ initialized per paper formula (0.20→0.67 across heads), trained with AdamW. Cancels common-mode attention noise, letting each head focus on differential signal. On clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, head_dim=128).

Raise Muon momentum peak from 0.97→0.98 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): empirical trend shows 0.97>0.95 (exp_089, −0.010), testing if 0.98 continues the improvement. Peak 0.98 gives effective memory window 1/(1−0.98)=50 steps vs 33 at 0.97, providing stronger gradient-direction averaging. Warmdown endpoint stays at 0.90 (empirically best); warmup updated to 0.85→0.98 over 400 steps.

Raise --embedding-lr from 0.3→0.4 (+33%) on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): the wte input embedding and value_embeds (at 0.5× embedding_lr) have never had their LR tuned across all 25+ opt experiments. Higher embedding LR could accelerate token representation learning — the embedding is the input interface to all 12 transformer layers and its gradient signal compounds through the entire network.

Round-robin multi-file interleaving in _document_batches: instead of exhausting one parquet file before the next, read one RG from each file in turn, cycling across all ~168 training shards. At ratio=130 (~1.4 epochs), every buffer fill draws from many corpus sections simultaneously, eliminating long-range within-file correlations. Different from exp_069 (staggered start-file per rank but sequential within) and exp_100 (shuffled RG order within one file).

Differential Attention (DIFF Transformer ICLR 2025): split Q,K into halves (D//2=64), batch both groups into ONE flash_attn call (2H=12 half-dim heads, v duplicated) to avoid 2× kernel-launch overhead that caused exp_143 timeout. y=y1-λ*y2, per-head λ initialized 0.20→0.67 (paper formula), trained with AdamW. ISO-parameter, effectively ISO-FLOP. On clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, head_dim=128).

Targeted AdamW β₂ ramp in last 10% of training only (step 90%→100%), from initial values (0.95–0.996) linearly to 0.999. exp_139 tried this over the full 65% warmdown (step 35%→100%) and got −0.002 within noise. Concentrating the increase in the final ~2700 steps (where LR is 5–15% of peak) is more targeted: at very low LR, gradient fluctuations dominate second-moment updates, making a slower EMA (higher β₂) most valuable. Clean rebase from exp_025.

Reduce Muon Polar Express ns_steps from 5→4 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): with 4 iterations SVs converge to ~99% of unit vs 99.9% with 5, preserving more gradient SV structure so dominant directions get proportionally stronger updates. Opposite direction from failed exp_049 (5→7 was worse; 5→4 is unexplored).

Add fullgraph=True to torch.compile(model) in base_train.py: forward is fully traceable (all conditionals are compile-time constants with dynamic=False), matching the existing compilation style of adamw_step_fused and muon_step_fused (both already use fullgraph=True). Eliminates any hidden graph breaks in the model forward, enabling better cross-op kernel fusion and consistent single-graph compilation of model + optimizer.

Paragraph splitting for long documents before BOS-bestfit packing: documents >7000 chars (~2100 tokens at 3.3 ch/tok) are split at \\n\\n boundaries into semantically complete paragraphs (each ≥300 chars), each treated as an independent BOS-prefixed document. Recovers tail content from long documents otherwise discarded by best-fit cropping, while keeping splits at natural paragraph boundaries (not arbitrary byte-level cuts like exp_060/135).

Add V-norm (normalize value vectors to unit RMS per head after VE mixing, before attention): Q and K are already normalized; V-norm completes QKV normalization, decouples attention output scale from c_v init, and makes each head output ≈ unit-scale (attn weights sum to 1, each v is unit RMS). Zero extra params, negligible compute. Motivated by HybridNorm / QKV-norm literature (2025).

Raise Muon momentum warmdown endpoint from 0.90→0.93 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): prior data — 0.85=-0.022, 0.90=best(0.2241), 0.97_const=-0.014 — shows a valley at 0.90. 0.93 (less reduction, 0.04 delta vs 0.07) is the only untested point in the less-penalized direction. Single-line code change in get_muon_momentum.

Set resid_lambdas weight_decay from 0.05→0.0 on clean exp_025 base: resid_lambdas initialize at 1.05-1.15 but AdamW WD decays toward 0, creating a bias below true optimum (at optimum the gradient≈0 so WD is uncontested). Removing WD lets them converge to their true optimal values.

Revert exp_151 paragraph splitting (hurt −0.028) and reduce best-fit buffer_size from 1000→100: smaller buffer means docs in each batch come from a narrower corpus window (~100K vs ~2M chars), creating more locally-coherent gradient signal. Observed pattern: buffer 1000→2000 gave −0.025 (exp_056), consistent with larger buffer = more temporal mixing = noisier gradients. Going below 1000 for the first time to test the opposite direction.

Add learnable logit_bias (32768 params, zero-init) applied after lm_head before softcap, on clean exp_025 base (V-norm from exp_152 removed). Decouples unigram token prior from residual stream: model can learn to suppress rare/never-seen tokens without wasting a residual direction on base-rate distribution. Inspired by modded-nanogpt speedrun 'logit shift' technique. Training duration unchanged (excluded from scaling_params like VE).

Add learnable per-feature output norm scale (ln_f_scale, 768 params, ones-init) applied after final RMSNorm before lm_head. Targeted variant of exp_072 (all-norms, −0.012): output norm is the only site where a learnable scale adds expressiveness without competing with attention QK dynamics or MLP c_fc gradients. Gives lm_head a direct 'feature importance' gradient path that AdamW on lm_head weights alone cannot provide as efficiently.

Add learnable per-layer attention temperature (qk_scale, 12 params, init=1.2) in CausalSelfAttention: replace hardcoded q*1.2/k*1.2 with learned per-layer scalar. Routed to AdamW (not Muon) at lr=0.01. Each layer learns its optimal attention sharpness; earlier layers may soften while later layers sharpen. exp_036 tried per-head per-layer (72 params, -0.024) — this is 6x more constrained and more stable.

Skip all intermediate val_bpb (eval-every=-1) and CORE evals (core-metric-every=99999, final still triggers via last_step) to reclaim ~20 min of eval overhead at ratio=130, redirecting that budget to 42% more training tokens (ratio 130→185). On exp_156 base (logit_bias). Estimated: 81.8 min pure training + 1 min final CORE ≈ 83 min total.

Raise logit_bias LR from 0.008 (unembedding_lr) to 0.04 and beta2 from 0.96 to 0.99 on exp_156 base: logit_bias targets the near-stationary log-unigram prior, so 5x faster LR lets it converge early and free residual capacity for the conditional distribution; smoother beta2=0.99 gives well-calibrated steps throughout.

Increase BOS-bestfit buffer_size 1000→4000 on clean exp_156 base: 4× more candidates dramatically increases probability of finding a short doc to fill small remaining row slots, reducing the ~35% token crop rate. More complete documents seen per step improves training signal quality. CPU overhead stays well below GPU step time (~20ms vs ~170ms/step). Previous buffer tests (exp_155 buf=100, exp_160 buf=1000) were on a dirty baseline; this is the first clean test at 4000.

Add learnable per-layer attention temperature (qk_scale, 12 params, init=1.2) in CausalSelfAttention: replace hardcoded q*1.2/k*1.2 with learned per-layer scalar cast to activation dtype. Routed to AdamW (not Muon) at lr=0.01. Each layer learns its optimal attention sharpness; earlier layers may soften while later layers sharpen. exp_036 tried per-head per-layer (72 params, -0.024) — this is 6x more constrained and more stable.

Skip all intermediate val_bpb (eval-every=-1) and CORE evals (core-metric-every=99999) to reclaim eval overhead, redirecting budget to 25% more training tokens (ratio 130→162). On exp_156 base (logit_bias). Calibrated from exp_163 overrun at ratio=185; ratio=162 estimated ~79 min total.

Apply C-AdamW cautious masking to adamw_step_fused: only update elements where exp_avg and grad agree in sign (mask = exp_avg * grad >= 0). Based on exp_156 (ratio=130, logit_bias). Prevents Adam from taking steps the current gradient contradicts; paper shows consistent LLM pretraining speedup.

Skip val_bpb evals (eval-every=-1, saves ~7-8 min) and redirect budget to ratio 130→140 (+7.7% tokens) on clean exp_156 base. val_bpb is diagnostic only and doesn't affect training dynamics. CORE evals retained at core-metric-every=2000. Estimated wall ~84 min.

Clip documents to row_capacity (T+1=2049 tokens) before buffering: docs longer than row_capacity can NEVER be placed whole in any row position (remaining space ≤ row_capacity always), so they accumulate as zombie buffer slots — only exiting when they happen to be the shortest doc in the buffer and get cropped to a tiny random prefix fragment. Pre-clipping converts these zombies into valid full-row-filler docs that are placed at pos=0 of a fresh row in clean complete BOS context (model sees first 2048 tokens of each long doc). Improves buffer diversity by eliminating dead slots; reduces pathological tiny-crop events. Rebased clean from exp_156.

Key offset in attention: second half of each head's key dims at position t ← position t-1's key values (after QK-norm+scale). Creates (current, prev-token) composite keys that architecturally enable induction-head patterns without any learned parameters. Zero-param change; causally valid; skipped for T=1 decode. Inspired by modded-nanogpt Record 58+ architecture.

Change final-lr-frac from 0.05→0.0 on clean exp_156 base: true linear decay-to-zero for the 65% warmdown phase, motivated by arXiv:2502.15938 showing linear D2Z consistently outperforms other schedules at compute-optimal training.

Raise lm_head and logit_bias AdamW beta2 from 0.96→0.98 on clean exp_156 base: lm_head receives cross-entropy gradients over 32K vocab, making its second moment noisy batch-to-batch; beta2=0.98 gives 50-step EMA window vs 25 steps at 0.96, stabilizing adaptive step sizes. Logit_bias shares the output pathway so gets the same change for consistency. Embeddings already use 0.995; lm_head at 0.98 bridges the gap.

Replace relu^2 MLP with SwiGLU (8/3 expansion, same param+FLOP count): 3*(n_embd*(8/3)*n_embd) = 2*(n_embd*4*n_embd) = 8*n_embd^2. Also reverts discarded exp_173 key-offset code. Gating adds multiplicative capacity at zero extra cost.

Mask loss at cross-document boundary positions (set target=-1, ignore_index=-1 in gpt.py): the last token of each packed doc no longer predicts the next BOS. This removes ~0.3% of tokens from training (the artificial "end-of-doc-A → BOS" transitions) to prevent the model from learning patterns that never appear at inference. Also reverts exp_172's harmful document clipping. Motivated by arxiv:2402.13991 (intra-document causal masking improves downstream quality).

Add mode="max-autotune-no-cudagraphs" to torch.compile: Inductor benchmarks GEMM/attention kernel implementations for our fixed shapes (768-dim, 2048-seq, 32 DBS) and selects the fastest, targeting 5-15% per-step speedup on GPU BF16. Restored ratio=130 and eval-every=250 to match exp_156 conditions exactly (ratio=140 trials consistently degrade quality due to logit_bias overfitting at longer horizons).

Add AdamW β₂ warmdown (initial→0.999 linearly during warmdown) on clean exp_156 base: at low LR, gradient noise dominates and higher β₂ smooths variance estimates. Stores initial_beta2 per group at optimizer creation, then ramps each AdamW group's β₂ toward 0.999 over the 65% warmdown phase. Also restores final-lr-frac 0.0→0.05 (revert exp_174). exp_139 tested this from exp_025 (−0.002, closest-to-threshold discard); testing from exp_156 with logit_bias group (β₂=0.96) which is new and may interact differently during convergence.

Remove cautious WD mask from muon_step_fused: switch from mask=(g*params>=0) gated WD to standard decoupled WD. Post-Polar Express, the orthogonalized gradient direction is less informative for sign-agreement tests than raw gradients, making unconditional WD cleaner and more principled.

Extend value embeddings (VE/ResFormer) from alternating 6 layers to all 12 layers: change has_ve() to always return True, so every attention block gets direct token-identity in the value stream (not just layers 1,3,5,7,9,11). Also reverts SwiGLU regression from exp_176 back to relu^2 MLP. VE params excluded from scaling_params so training token count unchanged (ratio=130 still drives iterations). Clean exp_156 base.

Revert exp_177's cross-document boundary loss masking: remove all_bos_positions tracking + cpu_targets masking loop from dataloader.py. exp_177 masked 0.3% of tokens at doc boundaries (set target=-1 before each non-first BOS) to avoid learning "last_tok_of_doc → BOS" patterns, but that experiment scored 0.2034 vs best 0.2244 (−0.021). Removing the masking restores pure BOS-bestfit packing on clean exp_156 base.

Revert max-autotune-no-cudagraphs compile regression (exp_178 added it, −0.021 vs exp_156). PyTorch 2.9.1 Inductor already auto-fuses BF16 cast+matmul (prologue fusion) and relu^2 epilogue by default — no manual config needed. The autotune mode added ~3 min kernel-benchmarking overhead and consistently regressed quality across exp_083/120/178 (each ~−0.020), likely by reducing effective training steps. Default torch.compile(dynamic=False) restores the proven exp_156 compile config.

Increase --warmup-steps from 40→200 on clean exp_156 base: at ratio=130 (≈27,274 steps), 40-step warmup is only 0.15% of training — far shorter than the standard 0.5–2% recommendation. The Muon momentum warmup already runs 400 steps; aligning LR warmup to 200 steps reduces the mismatch and gives a more gradual LR ramp during early Muon optimization. No code changes, pure flag.

Raise value_embeds AdamW LR from 0.5×→1.0× embedding_lr (0.15→0.30) on clean exp_156 base: value_embeds are ResFormer residuals of shape (vocab_size, kv_dim) — structurally identical to wte yet trained at half the embedding LR with no clear justification. At ratio=130 (~27K steps) this conservative haircut may leave value_embeds systematically under-adapted vs wte. Matching their LRs allows them to converge on equal footing. exp_084 tested the same change on exp_025 base (−0.0037, within noise floor ~0.005); on exp_156 base the dynamics with logit_bias may shift the outcome.

Add post-norm (sandwich norm) to Block.forward: wrap each sub-layer output with norm() before residual add — norm(attn(norm(x))) and norm(mlp(norm(x))). Follows Gemma-2 / OLMo-2 design validated at scale. Zero-init c_proj means norm(0)=0 so init is unchanged. No new parameters (F.rms_norm is parameter-free). Stabilizes per-block gradient magnitude through residuals.

Enable FP8 tensorwise training (--fp8) on exp_156 base, excluding lm_head from FP8 conversion to protect vocab gradient quality. exp_004 (ratio=12) hurt by -0.017, but ratio=130 is well-converged: gradients are stable and quantization noise is smaller relative to signal. relu^2 activation (vs SwiGLU) generates fewer outliers, reducing FP8 error. lm_head (768→32768) kept BF16 since its backward GEMM accumulates gradients over 32K vocab — most quality-critical matmul. All 72 attention/MLP linears use FP8 for ~30% step speedup.

Add document tail recycling: when a document is cropped to fill the remaining row space, prepend BOS to the tail (≥64 tokens) and add it back to the buffer as a BOS-aligned pseudo-document. Recovers ~35% of tokens discarded by crop events — these tail tokens were valid text but were thrown away. BOS prefix preserves the packing invariant (every placed segment starts with BOS). Recycled tails displace fresh parquet reads only marginally (~1 tail per row), preserving near-100% fresh-data diversity while boosting per-document completeness.

Switch Muon weight-decay schedule from cosine-decay-to-zero to constant (weight_decay_scaled) on clean exp_156 base: the current schedule drops WD to 0 exactly at the final training step, leaving the model unregularized during late warmdown convergence; constant WD maintains L2 regularization throughout and may improve CORE-metric generalization.

Raise Muon NorMuon variance-reduction beta2 from 0.9→0.95 on clean exp_156 base: the NorMuon paper (arXiv 2510.05491, cited in optim.py) recommends β2=0.95 for the factored second-moment EMA; the current 0.9 adapts too fast, producing a noisy per-neuron scale. Smoother variance estimate should stabilise the adaptive LR, especially during warmdown where gradients are already decaying.

Differential Attention (arXiv:2410.05258, ICLR 2025): split Q,K,V along head_dim into halves (64-dim each), compute 4 SDPA calls with matching dims (flash backend), combine as y=(attn1-λ*attn2) where λ=sigmoid(diff_lambdas[i]) per layer. Paper shows ~35% token-efficiency gain. Depth-dependent init λ: 0.20→0.79 (matching paper's λ_init schedule). +12 scalar params. 4×attention kernel calls keeps flash backend; ~15% wall-time overhead estimated.

Reduce warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.50 and change LR warmdown from linear to sqrt shape on exp_156 base: sqrt decay (LR = final_lr_frac + (1-final_lr_frac)*sqrt(progress)) stays near peak LR longer during warmdown then drops sharply at the end, giving more effective stable-phase equivalent training; shorter warmdown-ratio also increases time at full LR. WSD dynamics research finds power-law decay shapes competitive; both changes push toward less aggressive early annealing.

Reduce warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.50 and change LR warmdown from linear to sqrt shape on exp_156 base: sqrt decay (LR = final_lr_frac + (1-final_lr_frac)*sqrt(progress)) stays near peak LR longer during warmdown then drops sharply at the end, giving more effective stable-phase equivalent training; shorter warmdown-ratio also increases time at full LR. WSD dynamics research finds power-law decay shapes competitive; both changes push toward less aggressive early annealing.

Add per-head attention output gate (12×n_head Linear, AdamW) on clean exp_156 base: sigmoid gate conditioned on first 12 dims of pre-norm residual stream scales each head's output independently. Directly from modded-nanogpt PR #117 (−55 steps WR). Gate suppresses BOS attention-sink and learns head importance per token. 864 extra params, AdamW not Muon (asymmetric matrix).

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (half-cosine from 1.0→final_lr_frac over warmdown_ratio=0.65 of training) on clean exp_156 base: cosine maintains ~15-20% higher effective LR through first 60% of the warmdown phase vs linear, then decays more steeply in the final 40%, giving the model more learning time at high LR before convergence. All other args unchanged (ratio=130, warmup=40, final-lr-frac=0.05).

Reduce --eval-tokens 10× (80→8 ×524288) to reclaim ~6-10 min of val-bpb eval overhead, redirecting budget to ratio 130→155 (+19% training tokens) on clean exp_156 base. Eval reduction has no training-quality effect; 4M tokens gives SE<0.0001 on val_bpb. Log-linear scaling law (calibrated on ratio 100→130, +0.021) predicts +0.014 at 130→155.

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (half-cosine from 1.0→final_lr_frac over warmdown_ratio=0.65) on exp_156 base: cosine maintains ~15% higher effective LR through first 60% of warmdown then decays more steeply at end, giving more training time near peak LR before convergence. Formula: final_lr_frac + (1-final_lr_frac)*0.5*(1+cos(π*(1-progress))). All other args unchanged (ratio=130, warmup=40, final-lr-frac=0.05). Retry of exp_195 which crashed due to the infrastructure sync infrastructure failure, not code.

Reduce --eval-tokens 10× (80→8×524288, saves ~6-10 min) and redirect budget to ratio 130→155 (+19% tokens, ~32K steps): exp_196 had identical changes but crashed on the infrastructure failure. Resubmit clean config.

Add stateless MuonEq-R/C pre-orthogonalization equilibration to muon_step_fused: for tall matrices normalize columns (polar express uses column Gram X^TX), for wide/square normalize rows (X X^T), before the Frobenius normalization + polar express. This makes the Gram matrix fed into the 5-iteration polynomial closer to identity, yielding a more accurate quasi-unitary output. Keeps post-polar-express NorMuon intact. arXiv:2603.28254 (MuonEq) shows pre-equilibration outperforms post-rescaling (NorMuon) on 130M/350M LLM pretraining; this adds it as a complementary step on top of NorMuon.

Add stateless MuonEq-R/C pre-orthogonalization equilibration to muon_step_fused: for tall matrices normalize columns (polar express uses column Gram X^TX), for wide/square normalize rows (X X^T), before the Frobenius normalization + polar express. This makes the Gram matrix fed into the 5-iteration polynomial closer to identity, yielding a more accurate quasi-unitary output. Keeps post-polar-express NorMuon intact. arXiv:2603.28254 (MuonEq) shows pre-equilibration outperforms post-rescaling (NorMuon) on 130M/350M LLM pretraining; this adds it as a complementary step on top of NorMuon.

Rebase to clean exp_025 state: reverts exp_150 fullgraph=True regression (−0.021) and accumulated PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF fix from exp_142 (−0.017). Restores best-known sys recipe: BF16, ratio=130, window=L, default torch.compile mode.

Couple WD decay to LR warmdown: hold WD constant at peak (weight_decay_scaled) during the constant-LR phase, then cosine-decay from peak→0 only during warmdown — instead of global cosine decay across all training (which wastes ~42% WD by warmdown_start at step 35%).

Schedule NorMuon beta2 from 0.9→0.95 linearly during warmdown (last 65% of training): keep fast variance adaptation (beta2=0.9) during warmup/constant phase, stabilise to 0.95 at end so decaying-LR steps are guided by stable second-moment estimates.

Restore buffer_size from 100→1000: revert exp_155's harmful reduction (got 0.2013 vs 0.2241). The only structural diff from exp_025 in the current workdir; literature (arXiv:2511.18903) confirms data ordering changes are ineffective during LR warmdown, and at ratio=130 all 37% of epoch-2 data falls entirely in warmdown — explaining why all 20+ data experiments have been discards. Restoring the known-best data config to cleanly re-establish the exp_025 baseline for the data domain.

Revert broken exp_158 WD schedule (hold-then-cosine, core=0.188) back to exp_025's global cosine WD, and probe --target-param-data-ratio=135 (midpoint between 130=keep/0.2241 and 140=-0.003); exp_121's ratio=133 result (0.2087) was likely dirty-workdir noise.

Combine warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.70 + AdamW β₂ warmdown schedule (initial→0.999 linearly during warmdown) on clean exp_025 base: both changes individually were closest-to-best discards (−0.0018 and −0.0022 respectively); the combination is untested and may be synergistic since longer warmdown gives β₂ ramp more steps to stabilize variance estimates at low LR.

Enable value embeddings (ResFormer-style) on ALL 12 layers instead of alternating 6. has_ve() now returns True unconditionally, doubling VE coverage with negligible compute overhead: matmul FLOPs and training iterations are unchanged (VE params excluded from scaling_params), only embedding lookups add cost. Gives every layer direct access to token-level residuals, potentially alleviating attention concentration in odd layers that previously lacked VEs.

Rebase to ratio=100 (longer training), revert Muon LR formula to asymmetric (fix exp_023's harmful symmetric change), and increase NorMuon beta2 from exp_020's 0.9 to 0.95 for smoother per-neuron variance adaptation over the ~20k-step training horizon at ratio=100.
Increase target-param-data-ratio from 100→130 on top of exp_020's window=L base: 30% more training tokens, estimated ~78 min wall (exp_020 at ratio=100 finished in ~60 min).

GQA 2:1: reduce n_kv_head from 6→3 (num_heads//2) while keeping n_head=6. SDPA already handles GQA via enable_gqa=True. Forces 3 query-heads to share each KV-head (regularization), shrinks c_k/c_v ~7M params (→6% fewer training tokens, faster run), and reduces value_embed tables from kv_dim=768→384 (large VRAM saving).

Fix asymmetric Muon LR scaling: wide matrices (MLP c_proj 768×3072) got only 1× LR while tall matrices (c_fc 3072×768) got 2×; change to (max_dim/min_dim)^0.5 for all shapes, equalising per-element update magnitude across Q/K/V/O (sq), c_fc (tall), and c_proj (wide).
Revert Muon NorMuon beta2 to 0.9 (undo discarded exp_003) + raise lm_head AdamW betas from (0.8, 0.96) to (0.9, 0.99) for more stable variance tracking on the 32768-dim unembedding layer.
Revert MLP to relu^2 (4x, relu(x).square()) from failed SwiGLU exp_002; restore full-scale c_fc init; increase logit softcap 15→20 (nanochat LOG confirms 20 optimal at d24).
Shorten warmdown from 65% to 20% of training (research-backed: optimal WSD decay phase should be brief) + revert LR warmdown to linear (D2Z): spend 80% of compute at peak LR instead of 35%.

Train 7.5x longer (target-param-data-ratio 12→90) to fill the ~80-min available compute budget; model used only ~6% of ClimbMix at ratio=12 and val_bpb was still dropping rapidly at end. Also reverts exp_005's harmful tail-recovery packing.
